license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bu73nzqn
title: Confocal imaging power settings – protocol and discussion
author: Marco Polin

steps_raw.instruction: Use sample to set your microscope up for correct laser and scanning settings.
Make sure you use a calibrated power-meter and that it is zeroed before measurement and set to the correct wavelength matching that of your laser.
Remove sample and place power meter on to the sample holder or place power-meter slide, in case you are using a power-meter embedded in a slide.
Turn laser on and record power measurement.
Repeat measurement few times to make sure you get an average / approx. value at andaround the specimen position.
BE AWARE of lasers , and do not look into light path, while holding the power-meter in place.
Record laser power value and perform experiment.
At end of experiment, repeat 3 to 8 again and record laser power for end of experiment.
